title: Wire DS module to existing progress tracking — call markFeatureExplored
description:
module-progress.ts already defines 8 DS features (array-operations, linked-list, hash-table, bst-operations, heap-operations, bloom-filter, skip-list, disjoint-set) but markFeatureExplored is NEVER called from DataStructuresModule.tsx. The /modules page progress bar permanently shows 0% for Data Structures regardless of usage.

Fix: Call markFeatureExplored('data-structures', featureId) when user performs 3+ operations on a DS. Expand the feature map from 8 to all 34 DS. This is the lowest-effort retention feature — the system exists, just needs wiring.

Evidence: module-progress.ts line 59 defines the map. DataStructuresModule.tsx has zero calls to any progress function.
acceptanceCriteria: markFeatureExplored called after 3+ operations on any DS
All 34 DS mapped in module-progress feature list (not just 8)
/modules page shows accurate progress percentage for DS module
Progress persists across page refreshes via existing zustand-persist

title: Wire DS module to existing achievement system — trigger DS-specific achievements
description:
achievements.ts (652 lines) has 32 achievements with XP rewards, streak milestones, and rarity tiers. NONE fire from DS module interactions. The notification store supports 'achievement' type notifications.

Add DS-specific achievement triggers:
- 'ds-explorer': Explore 5 different data structures
- 'ds-collector': Explore all 34 data structures
- 'tree-master': Explore all 14 tree structures
- 'system-architect': Explore all 6 system design structures
- 'first-insert': Perform your first DS operation

Wire checkAchievements into handleOperation callback.

Evidence: achievements.ts exists at src/lib/interview/achievements.ts. notification-store.ts at src/stores/notification-store.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 5 DS-specific achievements defined
Achievements fire when conditions are met during DS operations
Notification toast appears when achievement unlocked
Achievements visible in existing achievement UI (if any)
XP awarded for DS achievements

title: Add streak counter for DS module using existing SRS infrastructure
description:
srs.ts (287 lines) already implements FSRS with getRetentionStats computing streakDays. The Interview module uses this. DS module has zero streak tracking.

Add a lightweight streak counter: track daily DS usage in localStorage. Show streak in sidebar header ('🔥 5 day streak'). Streak increments when user performs any operation on any DS. Streak freezes available (1 per week). Use the existing notification system to show streak milestones.

Research: Duolingo users with 7-day streaks are 3.6x more likely to stay engaged long-term. Streak wagers boost day-14 retention by 14%.

Source: Duolingo retention mechanics research, Lenny's Newsletter.
acceptanceCriteria: Streak counter visible in DS sidebar header
Streak increments on daily DS usage
Streak persists in localStorage
Streak milestone notifications at 3, 7, 14, 30 days
Streak freeze available (1 per week, auto-applied)

title: Add individual URL routes for all 34 data structures (/ds/[slug])
description:
ZERO of 34 data structures have crawlable URLs. Everything lives at '/' as client-side state. Google cannot index any DS content. Competitors like algorithm-visualizer.org rank #1 because they have individual URLs.

Create: src/app/ds/[slug]/page.tsx that renders the DS module with the specified DS pre-selected. URL examples:
- /ds/bloom-filter
- /ds/binary-search-tree
- /ds/consistent-hash-ring

The slug maps to DS_CATALOG.id. Use Next.js generateStaticParams to pre-render all 34 pages. Each page gets unique meta title/description from the catalog.

Evidence: src/app/sitemap.ts generates URLs for problems, concepts, patterns — but zero for DS. The OG image system supports custom types but 'data-structure' is not one of them.
acceptanceCriteria: All 34 DS accessible at /ds/[slug] URLs
Each page has unique <title> and <meta description>
Pages are server-rendered (SSR/SSG) for SEO
generateStaticParams pre-renders all 34 pages
Existing sidebar navigation still works
Direct URL loads correct DS visualization

title: Add DS pages to sitemap.ts and generate JSON-LD structured data
description:
sitemap.ts lists problems, blog posts, concepts, patterns, LLD, company pages — zero DS pages. JSON-LD infrastructure exists (generateLearningResourceJsonLd in seo/json-ld.ts) but is never called for DS.

Add all 34 DS to sitemap.ts using the /ds/[slug] routes (requires DST-169). Generate JSON-LD LearningResource schema for each DS page with: name, description, educationalLevel, teaches, timeRequired.

Research: Pages with structured data get 30% more clicks (BrightEdge). GPT-4 accuracy goes from 16% to 54% with structured content.

Source: searchengineland.com structured data 2025, digidop.com structured data AI.
acceptanceCriteria: All 34 DS pages in sitemap.ts
Each DS page has JSON-LD LearningResource schema
Schema includes name, description, educationalLevel from DS_CATALOG
Google Rich Results Test passes for DS pages
AI search engines can parse DS content from structured data

title: Add Open Graph images and social sharing for DS pages
description:
OG image system exists at src/app/api/og/route.tsx supporting concept/problem/pattern types but NOT 'data-structure'. Sharing any DS page shows generic Architex preview — no DS-specific image.

Add 'data-structure' type to OG image generator. Auto-generate preview image showing: DS name, category badge, complexity summary, and a mini visualization thumbnail. Add Share button to DS controls bar that copies the /ds/[slug] URL with current state encoded.

For social virality: the OG image should look beautiful enough that someone would share it on LinkedIn/Twitter just because it looks good.

Research: Native format content (images, short video) get 3-5x more engagement than external links on social platforms.
acceptanceCriteria: Each DS page has unique Open Graph image
OG image shows DS name, category, and complexity summary
Share button copies URL with current DS state
Shared links render beautiful preview cards on Twitter/LinkedIn
OG image auto-generated (not manually created per DS)

title: Create DS template generator CLI for adding new data structures
description:
Adding a new DS requires 9 mandatory edits across 2-3 files with zero scaffolding. Estimated 6-14 hours per DS. Other modules have guides (adding-an-algorithm.md, adding-a-node.md) but DS has none.

Create a CLI script: `pnpm run add-ds --name 'Cuckoo Hash' --id cuckoo-hash --category hash`

This should:
1. Create src/lib/data-structures/cuckoo-hash.ts with type stubs and operation function signatures
2. Add exports to index.ts barrel
3. Add DSConfig entry to DS_CATALOG with placeholder description
4. Add type to ActiveDS union
5. Add state field to DSModuleState
6. Add initial state to INITIAL_STATE
7. Add operation handler case stub
8. Add random handler case stub
9. Add reset handler field
10. Add canvas component stub
11. Create test file stub

This reduces add-DS time from 6-14 hours to 2-4 hours.

Evidence: 9 locations documented in operations audit.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI generates all required file stubs from name + id + category
Generated code compiles without errors
New DS appears in sidebar after generation
All 9 mandatory locations are scaffolded
Test file stub created with basic test structure

title: Write 'Adding a Data Structure' content author guide
description:
docs/guides/ has guides for adding-a-module.md, adding-a-node.md, adding-an-algorithm.md, adding-a-template.md — but NO adding-a-data-structure.md. Content contributors have zero reference documentation.

Write comprehensive guide covering:
1. Architecture overview (engine file → index.ts → DataStructuresModule.tsx)
2. Step-by-step instructions for all 9 mandatory change locations
3. Naming conventions (file naming, function prefixes, state field names)
4. Quality checklist (what makes a DS 'done')
5. Example walkthrough: adding a new DS end-to-end
6. Step description guidelines (WHY not just WHAT)
7. Visualization guidelines (colors, animation, metaphor)
8. Testing requirements

Use the heap-ds.ts implementation as the reference 'gold standard' example (highest quality in the module per implementation audit).
acceptanceCriteria: Guide exists at docs/guides/adding-a-data-structure.md
Covers all 9 mandatory change locations with file:line references
Includes quality checklist with minimum requirements
References heap-ds.ts as the gold standard implementation
A developer could follow the guide to add a new DS without asking questions

title: Add embeddable widget — iframe code for DS visualizations in blog posts
description:
Zero embed/export features exist for DS. Content creators and professors need to embed interactive DS visualizations in their blog posts, slides, and course websites.

Create an embed endpoint: /embed/ds/[slug] that renders ONLY the canvas + controls (no sidebar, no properties panel, no module chrome). Add an 'Embed' button in DS controls that shows the iframe code:

<iframe src='https://architex.dev/embed/ds/bloom-filter' width='600' height='400'></iframe>

The embed should be lightweight (~50KB), load fast, and be interactive (user can perform operations).

Research: Embeddable widgets drive organic discovery — every blog post embedding the widget is a backlink + traffic source.
acceptanceCriteria: Embed endpoint renders DS canvas only (no app chrome)
Embed button in controls shows copyable iframe code
Embedded visualization is interactive (insert/delete/search work)
Embed loads under 100KB and renders within 2 seconds
Works in common platforms (WordPress, Medium, Ghost, Notion)

title: Wire DS module to existing SRS for spaced repetition review prompts
description:
srs.ts implements full FSRS spaced repetition. When a student explores a DS and answers prediction questions (DST-149), create an SRS review card. After N days, prompt: 'You learned Bloom Filter 3 days ago — time to check your recall!'

This is the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve solution. Students forget 80% within a week without review. SRS schedules reviews at optimal intervals.

Wire: After completing a prediction challenge (DST-149) or manual trace (DST-150) for a DS, call createCard from srs.ts. On module load, check getDueCards — if any DS cards are due, show a review prompt banner.

Source: Kang 2016, meta-analysis showing Cohen's d = 0.54-0.80 for spaced retrieval practice.
acceptanceCriteria: SRS cards created when student completes DS practice activities
getDueCards checked on module load
Review prompt banner shown for due DS topics
Clicking review prompt opens the DS with a prediction challenge
SRS scheduling follows FSRS algorithm from existing srs.ts
